finding.description: The search method uses raw SQL queries, which can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities.
finding.recommendation: Use Eloquent ORM methods instead of raw SQL queries to prevent SQL injection.
tests: Test for SQL injection vulnerabilities in the search method.

finding.description: The Vendor model uses $fillable instead of $guarded, violating the mass assignment convention.
finding.recommendation: Change the model to use $guarded = ['id']; instead of $fillable.
tests: Test that mass assignment works correctly with the updated guarded property.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-02 for mass assignment conventions.

finding.description: The search view uses {!! !!} for outputting vendor names, which can lead to XSS vulnerabilities.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-03] XSS vulnerability in search view
finding.recommendation: Change {!! $vendor->name !!} to {{ $vendor->name }} to ensure proper escaping.
tests: Test that the search results are displayed without XSS vulnerabilities.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-03 for XSS prevention.

finding.description: The store method does not validate the request data using $request->validate().
finding.recommendation: Implement inline validation for the store method using $request->validate().
tests: Ensure validation errors are returned for invalid input.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the HasCreator and ActivityLogged traits, which are required for audit tracking.
finding.recommendation: Implement the HasCreator and ActivityLogged traits in the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the model correctly tracks creator and activity logs.
finding.notes: Refer to rules CTDL-07 and CTDL-08 for audit requirements.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the explicit $table property with the escm_ prefix, violating the database convention.
finding.recommendation: Add the property protected $table = 'escm_vendors'; to the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the model correctly references the escm_vendors table.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-10 for table naming conventions.

finding.description: The index method does not call checkAccessPermission() and does not use ESCM::scopeProject(auth()->user()) for multi-tenant isolation.
finding.recommendation: Add checkAccessPermission() and use ESCM::scopeProject(auth()->user()) in the index method.
tests: Verify that unauthorized users cannot access the index method.

finding.description: The syncFromSap method does not use a dedicated sync service and does not extend Base.
finding.recommendation: Create a dedicated sync service that extends Base for syncing from SAP.
tests: Ensure that the sync process is handled by a dedicated service.

finding.description: The store method logs sensitive data (password, pin) without masking.
finding.recommendation: Mask sensitive fields in the log before logging.
tests: Check logs to ensure sensitive data is not exposed.

fp_findings.description: The store method does not validate the incoming request data, which can lead to invalid data being saved to the database.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q02] Missing validation in store method
fp_findings.recommendation: Add validation to the store method using $request->validate([...]) to ensure data integrity.
tests: Test that invalid data cannot be saved to the database.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule WIKA-Q02 for validation requirements.

fp_findings.description: The search method uses a raw SQL query, which violates the convention of using Eloquent ORM for database access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query in search method
fp_findings.recommendation: Replace the raw SQL query with an Eloquent query using the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the search functionality works correctly using Eloquent.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-01 for database access conventions.

fp_findings.description: The export method does not check for user permissions before exporting vendor data, which can lead to unauthorized access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-11] Missing permission check in export method
fp_findings.recommendation: Implement a permission check at the start of the export method using checkAccessPermission().
tests: Test that unauthorized users cannot access the export functionality.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-11 for authorization requirements.

fp_findings.description: The detail method does not follow the Citadel page lifecycle.
fp_findings.recommendation: Follow the Citadel page lifecycle in the detail method.
tests: Ensure the page lifecycle is correctly implemented.

fp_findings.description: The callback method is vulnerable to open redirect attacks.
fp_findings.recommendation: Validate the redirect URL to prevent open redirect vulnerabilities.
tests: Test for open redirect vulnerabilities in the callback method.

findings.description: The search method uses raw SQL queries, which can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities.
findings.recommendation: Use Eloquent ORM methods instead of raw SQL queries to prevent SQL injection.

findings.description: The search method also uses DB::raw() which is not allowed unless for complex aggregations.
findings.recommendation: Replace DB::raw() with Eloquent ORM methods.
tests: Ensure that the stats calculation is done using Eloquent ORM.

findings.description: The update_status method does not check for permissions before updating the status.
findings.recommendation: Add permission checks at the start of the update_status method.
tests: Verify that unauthorized users cannot change the status.

findings.recommendation: Follow the Citadel page lifecycle in the detail method.

findings.recommendation: Add permission checks at the start of the exportAll method.
